topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into my afternoon. My heart gave a sudden, hard thump against my ribs. I clicked it open, my fingers hovering over the screen as if afraid to touch the reality of it. The headline was mine—my meticulously crafted analysis of the city council's new zoning proposal—but the byline beneath it read "J. Sterling." A cold wave washed over me, quickly followed by a sharp, almost frantic surge of energy. I scrambled to the source material, my breath catching in my throat. This wasn't just a mistake; this was a complete hijacking of my voice, my work. I needed to know who J. Sterling was, and I needed to fix this, now. I started drafting an email, my hands flying across the keyboard, the need to reclaim my narrative driving every keystroke.

[story 2]

I reread the digital proof, my eyes scanning the layout, the carefully chosen words. Then I saw it: the attribution. A sickening lurch in my stomach, instantly transmuted into a tight, almost buzzing feeling behind my eyes. I leaned back in my chair, the plastic groaning under my weight, and stared at the screen, my jaw tight. A slow, deliberate movement took over. I pulled up my contact list, my thumb tracing the name of the editor who had approved the final submission. A rapid succession of calls began, my voice pitched slightly higher than usual, buzzing with an urgent need to demand answers. Every second stretched thin; I couldn't sit still. I felt a distinct prickling sensation across my skin, a coiled tension demanding release.

[story 3]

When the email landed, the sheer audacity of it hit me like a physical blow. My initial reaction was a stunned silence, my gaze fixed on the screen, the words blurring for a moment. But the shock quickly morphed into something sharper, something demanding action. I pushed my chair back so fast it scraped loudly against the floor. My hands clenched into fists at my sides, the knuckles turning white. I began pacing the small room, a restless energy thrumming beneath my skin, a need to confront this injustice immediately. I started formulating a strategy, my mind racing through possibilities, every thought a sharp dart aimed at finding the loophole, the correction, the way to shatter this false attribution.
